seo.title: Privacy Policy | Science for Africa
seo.description: Privacy Policy, Terms of Use, and Community Guidelines for the Pan-African Community of Practice Platform.

title: Privacy Policy
subtitle: Pan-African Community of Practice for Research and Innovation Management
intro: The Pan-African Community of Practice for Research and Innovation Management (“the CoP”, “the Platform”) is committed to safeguarding personal data and upholding internationally recognised principles of data protection, privacy, and responsible digital governance.

aligned_with.title: This Privacy Policy is aligned with:
points: the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR),
African Union Data Protection and Digital Governance Principles, and
the accountability and transparency expectations of international public and philanthropic research funders.

scope.title: Scope of This Policy
scope.content: This policy applies to all users of the Platform, including individual members, institutional accounts, mentors, moderators, partners, and administrators.
scope.acknowledgment: By registering for or using the Platform, you acknowledge and accept the practices described in this policy.

principles.title: Principles Governing Our Use of Data
principles.intro: We process personal data in accordance with the following principles:
points: Lawfulness, fairness, and transparency
Purpose limitation (data used only for defined professional purposes)
Data minimisation (only data that is necessary is collected)
Accuracy and currency
Storage limitation
Integrity and confidentiality
Accountability and stewardship
principles.footer: These principles reflect both GDPR requirements and AU commitments to responsible, people-centred digital systems.

collection.title: Information We Collect
collection.intro: We may collect the following categories of data:

sections.title: Identity and Professional Information
points: Name, email address, country, institution, professional role
Career stage, areas of expertise, and interests
Institutional affiliation and verification status

sections.title: Platform and Participation Data
points: Posts, comments, resource uploads, and discussion participation
Collaboration, mentorship, and working-group activity
Learning enrolment, completion, badges, and certifications

sections.title: Technical and Usage Data
points: Log-in records, device and browser information
IP address and session metadata
Cookies and platform analytics data

collection.footer: You may choose how much profile information is visible to others through privacy controls.

lawful_basis.title: Lawful Basis for Processing
lawful_basis.intro: We process personal data under one or more of the following lawful bases:
points: Consent (e.g. account creation, communications)
Legitimate professional interest in operating a trusted, secure community
Performance of programme or platform functions
Compliance with legal and funder obligations
lawful_basis.footer: Users may withdraw consent at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing already undertaken.

usage.title: How We Use Your Information
points: Operate and improve the CoP platform
Enable professional interaction, collaboration, and mentorship
Deliver learning activities, events, and certification
Support professional recognition and identity
Personalise content, notifications, and recommendations
Monitor platform performance and engagement (aggregated and anonymised where possible)
Fulfil reporting and accountability obligations to funders and partners
usage.footer: We do not use personal data for commercial advertising.

sharing.title: Data Sharing and International Transfers
sharing.intro: We do not sell or monetise personal data.
points: with trusted service providers supporting platform operation;
with learning or certification partners (where relevant);
where required by law or regulatory authority;
in anonymised or aggregated form for reporting and ecosystem learning.
sharing.transfer_footer: Where data is processed or stored across borders, we apply safeguards consistent with GDPR and AU principles, including contractual and technical protections.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.intro: Personal data is retained only as long as necessary to:
points: provide platform services,
meet legal or funder reporting requirements,
protect platform integrity and auditability.
retention.footer: Users may request deletion of their data, subject to legal or operational constraints (e.g. audit logs, safeguarding records).

security.title: Security and Safeguards
security.intro: We implement proportionate technical and organisational measures, including:
points: encrypted storage and secure access controls;
role-based permissions;
audit trails for sensitive actions;
regular review of platform security and governance practices.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.intro: In line with GDPR and AU principles, you have the right to:
points: Access your personal data
Correct or update inaccurate data
Request deletion (“right to be forgotten”), where applicable
Restrict or object to certain processing
Request data portability
Withdraw consent
Lodge a complaint where required
rights.footer: Requests can be made using the contact details below.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect legal, regulatory, or operational changes.
changes.footer: Material updates will be communicated clearly on the Platform.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: For privacy or data-protection matters, please contact:
contact.email_value: [Insert Data Protection / Platform Contact Email - TBC]

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms of Use define the conditions under which the CoP platform is accessed and used.
terms.acceptance: By using the platform, you agree to these Terms.

purpose.title: Purpose and Values of the Platform
points: professionalise research and innovation management;
enable peer learning, collaboration, and mentorship;
support strong, ethical, and accountable research systems in Africa.
purpose.footer: Use of the Platform must align with these purposes and values.

registration.title: Registration and Account Responsibility
points: Users must provide accurate and current information
Accounts are personal and may not be shared
Institutional affiliations are subject to verification
The Platform reserves the right to approve, restrict, or suspend accounts

professional_use.title: Acceptable Professional Use
points: act lawfully, ethically, and professionally;
engage respectfully and constructively;
contribute in good faith to shared learning and collaboration;
respect confidentiality, intellectual property, and data rights.

prohibited.title: Prohibited Conduct
points: post harmful, misleading, or discriminatory content;
misuse data or breach confidentiality;
engage in unauthorised commercial activity;
undermine platform security, trust, or governance.

ugc.title: User-Generated Content
points: Users retain ownership of their content
By posting, users grant the Platform a non-exclusive licence to host and display it
Content may be moderated to uphold standards, safeguard users, and meet funder and legal obligations

governance.title: Platform Governance and Enforcement
points: moderate or remove content;
suspend or terminate access;
take necessary action to protect users, partners, and institutional integrity.
governance.footer: Serious or repeated violations may lead to permanent removal.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content: The Platform is provided on an “as-is” basis.
liability.footer: Participation does not guarantee outcomes, funding, or professional advancement.

law.title: Governing Law
law.content: These Terms are governed by applicable laws in the platform’s jurisdiction of operation (e.g. Kenya), alongside applicable international data and digital governance frameworks.

community_guidelines.title: Community Guidelines
community_guidelines.subtitle: Building a Trusted, Professional Community
community_guidelines.intro: The CoP is a professional, inclusive, and trusted space. These guidelines reflect shared responsibility and stewardship.

conduct.title: Professional Conduct
points: treat others with respect and fairness;
support learning across career stages;
acknowledge different institutional and national contexts;
engage constructively and responsibly.

sharing.title: Responsible Knowledge Sharing
points: share practice-based experience and evidence;
credit sources and collaborators;
distinguish opinion from fact;
avoid misinformation or unverified claims.

inclusion.title: Inclusion, Equity, and Safeguarding
points: harassment, discrimination, or exclusion;
abuse of power or status;
behaviour that undermines psychological or professional safety.

moderation.title: Moderation and Accountability
points: Moderators guide discussions and enforce standards
Decisions are documented and proportionate
Due process is applied, consistent with funder and governance expectations

reporting.title: Reporting and Shared Responsibility
points: maintaining trust and quality;
reporting concerns in good faith;
contributing to a professional research management community that strengthens Africa’s research and innovation systems.
